observations.content: User restarted the session and the agent responded with a brief, open-ended prompt to continue work.
observations.context: The user sent only 'Restarted' indicating they had restarted the session or context. The agent responded with 'Working. What do you want to dig into first?' which is a concise, ready-to-work acknowledgment. No actual task was initiated.
source_messages:
User: Restarted
Assistant: Working. What do you want to dig into first?

observations.content: Phantom runs on a Raspberry Pi (VM) accessible via SSH, not on the user's local machine.
observations.context: During the auth flow, the user pasted the OAuth code into their local Claude Code instance rather than the SSH terminal, revealing that Phantom runs on a remote Raspberry Pi. The agent had to clarify that credentials need to be on the Pi at ~/.claude/.credentials.json.
source_messages: Not here. The code was likely pasted into Claude Code running on your local machine, which created the credentials file there - not on this VM.

observations.content: The user wants to use Claude.ai subscription credits (OAuth tokens) instead of pay-as-you-go API keys for Phantom.
observations.context: The user asked about replicating the claude-code-rust setup, which uses OAuth credentials from a Claude.ai subscription rather than API keys. The agent proposed commenting out ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env and using ~/.claude/.credentials.json instead.
source_messages: Could we make same set up?

observations.content: User runs Phantom on a remote Raspberry Pi and interacts with it via SSH sessions.
observations.context: The auth confusion revealed that the user's local machine and the Phantom server are separate environments. The agent needs to be aware that interactive commands requiring browser flows or terminal input must be run in SSH sessions on the Pi.
source_messages: In an SSH session to this machine: bunx @anthropic-ai/claude-code auth login

observations.content: bunx can be used to run @anthropic-ai/claude-code without a global install on the Pi.
observations.context: The agent identified that bunx (Bun's npx equivalent) can run the claude-code package without installing it globally, which is useful for one-off auth flows on the Pi.
source_messages: bunx can run it. So we don't even need to install anything - bunx @anthropic-ai/claude-code auth login will work for the auth step.
